title: Positive End Expiratory Pressure (PEEP)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- For use with ventilated patients who meet PEEP criteria: 
- Cardiac arrest.
- Near drowning.
- Severe asthma/COPD exacerbation.
- Severe pulmonary oedema.
- Carbon monoxide poisoning.
- Intubated patients.

#### Procedure

Figure 1

- Attach the PEEP valve to the bag valve (see **Figure 1**).

- Start PEEP level at 5cm.

- Increase to 10cm if not responding to 5cmH2O (max for asthma, CAL, infants and children).

- Increase to 15cm as required for pulmonary oedema, near drowning and CO poisoning (only if still desaturated with 10 cmH2O).

- Maintain continuous PEEP.

#### Contraindications

- Suspected pneumothorax.

#### Precautions

- Conditions with suspicion of raised ICP, CVA, traumatic brain injury, etc.

#### Complications

- Hypotension – use cautiously in shocked patients.
- At higher PEEP values, decreased cardiac output or barotrauma can occur.
